a) A state entity, local entity, or law enforcement agency shall not discharge, threaten, or 1 otherwise discriminate or retaliate against any official, representative, agent, or employee for 2 reporting a known or probable violation of the provisions of this article to the Attorney General.
Allows State AG to sue cities and postsecondary campuses for having welcoming city ordinances.

directs the state police to work with local law enforcement agencies to collect data about all immigrants charged with crimes in Louisiana. Within the next six months, the Department of Public Safety and Corrections must find a way to regularly publish that data.
The order mandates that all state agencies come up with a plan to analyze and report to the governor public expenses that directly or indirectly benefit immigrants within four months
Must use e-verify for government contracts

a student who is a COFA islander and who has notpreviously established residence in any state or territory of the United States or the District of Columbia otherthan Oregon shall qualify for exemption from nonresident tuition and fees for enrollment as an undergraduatestudent and as a graduate student in a degree program at Oregon Health and Science University

Allows an otherwise qualified person who becomes United States citizen less than a specifiednumber of days before date of primary election to file for and be elected to office of precinct committee person,if person registers as member of major political party by filing deadline

Requires Oregon Department of Emergency Management, during certain emergencies, to prioritizeimmediate provision of housing to displaced individuals, ensures that such temporary housing is safe and that itis provided in compliance with state and federal laws relating to discrimination, including but not limited to laws relating to housing discrimination, public accommodation discrimination and discrimination in the provision ofgovernment programs and services.

Allows the Division of Motor Vehicles to issue a State identification card to residents unable toestablish lawful presence in the United States upon proof of proper documentation as required with respect todriver privilege cards and permits, provides that a state identification card so issued would not be valid forvoting purposes.

Provides that, with specified exceptions, an alien, resident, or nonresident of the United States, maytake and hold property, real or personal, in the State and dispose of or transmit the same as a native citizen,provides that, with exceptions, the heir or heirs, or devisee or devisees, of an alien, resident, or nonresident of theUnited States, may take lands, held by descent or otherwise, as if a citizen or citizens of the United States.

Relates to employment, relates to the Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements Program,provides that to be eligible for a professional license or permit issued pursuant to Title 57, Intoxicating Liquors,an applicant may show the that the applicant has a valid J-1 or F-1 student visa, as verified by the AlcoholicBeverage Commission.

Relates to a St. Jude Children's Research Hospital Global Collaboration License, provides that theBoard of Medical Examiners may issue a temporary license of limited duration to an international medicalschool graduate upon finding sufficient evidence that the international medical school graduate, among otherthings, demonstrated competency and a completed post-graduate training program in the graduate's licensingcountry.
CBP Officers with proper training can make arrests under Texas Law

Provides that on behalf of this State, the governor may develop and execute an interstate compact forborder security among interested states, provides that the compact may not increase the political power of thecompacting states in relation to the federal government, provides that the compact must provide for joint actionamong compacting states on specified matters.
Encourages cooperation with federal immigration officers

Provides that the district clerk may not charge a fee for a copy of any document on file or of recordin the clerk's office relating to an individual's criminal history, regardless of whether the document is certified, tothe United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement, United States Citizenship and Immigration Services,or a criminal justice agency that requests the document for a criminal justice purpose, including a request todetermine an individual's eligibility to purchase a firearm.
Relates to a study on enhancing border security outcomes through public safety, technological, andtransportation infrastructure improvements near State-Mexico border crossings.

Requires an institution within the State system of higher education to grant residency status to anindividual who is not a citizen of the United States but has been granted or has applied for certain immigrationstatus.

Allows the Driver License Division to begin administering certain examinations in languages otherthan English, allows an individual to take certain driver license examinations in the individual's preferredlanguage, subject to availability, for the individual's initial application and first renewal application for a driverlicense, allows a translator for certain driver license examinations in certain circumstances, requires a reportfrom the Driver License Division to the Transportation Interim Committee.

Modifies provisions related to the Governor's Office of Economic Opportunity, requires theGovernor's Office of Economic Opportunity to report certain information regarding reinvestment agencies to theLegislature, expands the nonvoting membership of the Unified Economic Opportunity Commission, expands themembership of the Unified Economic Opportunity Commission's Women in the Economy Subcommittee,provides for the Utah Center for Immigration and Integration and modifies the center's duties.
ositive provides healthcare to non-citizen kids if they cannot qualify for medicaid or CHIP because of status and their parent works
Restricts certain non-immigrants from possessing firearms.
Make funding available for refugee housing.
Created funding to support New Americans
Easier for foreign trained pschologist to get licensed

Revises the process for individuals to request name changes, provides that Name change petitionsmay be filed and shall be heard in any superior court in the state when a person desiring a change of the person'sname is an emancipated minor or has received asylum, refugee, or special immigrant juvenile status.
Expands the farm internship program
cannot consider immigration status for Working Connections Child Care Program eligibility

Relates to malpractice insurance for international medical graduate supervisors, provides that uponnomination by the chief medical officer of any hospital, appropriate medical practice located in the State, theDepartment of Social and Health Services, the Department of Children, Youth, and Families, the Department ofCorrections, or a county or city health department, the State Medical Commission may issue a limited license toan international medical graduate if the applicant meets certain requirements.

Provides that a State entity, local entity or law enforcement agency shall not adopt or maintain a law,ordinance, resolution, rule, regulation, policy, directive, order, practice, procedure, or custom, formal orinformal, written or unwritten, which prohibits or materially restricts the State entity, local entity, or lawenforcement agency from enforcing immigration laws.

Academic Program at the University of Texas at Austin. Served over 60 students per semester with scholarships, mentorship, and other advising. Provided specific, but not exclusive, support for undocumented students, students with temporary status, students from mixed-status families, and U.S. citizen allies. Ended in 2024 by the passing of SB 17.

After calls from the Mayor of Chicago to build more migrant shelters, Gov. J.B. Pritzker indicated additional sites should be located outside Chicago, even with state funding. Two new shelters opened in Chicago in June 2024 with state funding.
Governor included services for asylum seekers in her budget proposal. This inlcuded $500 million for asylum seekers and $2.4 billion for all immigrant services.

Governon wrote a letter to various bus companies participating in the dropping off of migrants in NJ. He requested that they give 32 hour notice, tell them who is on board and how many people there are, give the locations they will drop off passengers, describe the bus, and more.
The order requires all state agencies that offer voter registration forms to the public, as called for under federal law, to include notice that it is illegal for noncitizens to vote.

The order directs the state Office of Motor Vehicles (OMV) to compile a list of people who have been issued a temporary, 180-day Louisiana driver’s license or identification card. The agency will then provide the list to Secretary of State Nancy Landry, a Republican who is not related to the governor, so she can cross-check it against voter rolls.

Illinois announced the establishment of an intake center for migrants located in Streator, Il. The site will inclide heated tents and is an effort to help process the "holiday uptick" of asylum seekers.
Ordered the Governor's office to create a new state office for the economic and civic integration of migrants. Office was then created.
Allows for misdemeanor violations to lead to an arrest of any adult. Can then verify immigration status.
Allows taking DNA samples from detained individuals.
Mandates the use of an interpreter if the detained individual does not speak english.
Mandates Georgia officials to honor immigration detainers.
Mandates a withholding of federal funds for Georgia if they do not act in accordance to the law.
Prohibits creating any sanctuary policies and creates crimes for anyone who violates this law.
Prohibits creating any sanctuary policies in Louisiana.
Allows Louisiana officials to transport immigrants to federal custody
Prohibits Louisiana officials from detaining immigrants who are cooperating witnesses and victims. Also, it does not mandate officials to release information on those witnesses/victims.
Requires county jails to enter into agreements to house detainees subject to detainer requests.

Mandates all law enforcement and officials to communicate with federal agencies regarding the immigration status of individuals. must also cooperate with federal officials in the identification, apprehension, detention, and removal of aliens unlawfully present in the USA.
Creates a crime for unlawful entry/reentry into the state of Louisiana. First offense is 6 months imprisonment and/or $1,000.00 fine.

"No court shall abate or terminate the prosecution of an offense pursuant to this Subpart on teh basis that a federal determination regarding the immigration status of the defendant has not been determined or may possibly be initiated at a future date."
Creates an interstate compact for border security. Joining with other states in mutual assistance in order for border states to control the influx of illegal immigration.

Limits counties and municipalities from accepting certain documents as valid for identification if they do not denote a lawful presence. They also cannot use any money for purposes of making identification purposes unless they are limited to those lawfully present in the country.

Requiring the Maryland Health Benefit Exchange to establish and implement the Qualified Resident Enrollment Program to facilitate the enrollment of qualified residents in qualified plans; providing that the operation and administration of the Program may include functions delegated by the Maryland Exchange to a third party; and providing that the implementation of the Program is contingent on approval of a certain waiver application amendment.
Creates a crime for unlawful entry/reentry into the state of Iowa. Classifies it as an aggravated misdemeanor.

Creates "safe spaces" where peace officers may NOT arrest/detain a person for enforcing this law. Those are schools, places of worship, health care facilities, and facilities for medical examinations related to sexual assault.
"A court may not abate the prosecution of an offense under this chapter on the basis that a federal determination regarding the immigration status of the person is pending or will be initiated."
Allows immigrants to voluntarily depart.
Immigrants who refuse to comply with their order to return to their nation will receive a class C felony.
Indemnifies local government officials, employees, or contractors from liability arising from a cause of action under state law resulting from an action taken to enforce these laws.

Creates a third-degree felony (up to 5 years in prison) to be charged as a second-degree felony (up to 15 years in prison) for those who have previously been deported. Second-degree felonies (up to 15 years in prison) will now be charged as first-degree felonies (up to 30 years in prison). And first-degree felonies (up to 30 years in prison) will be charged as a life felonies for those individuals.

This law will increase penalties on those who operate a vehicle without a license by increasing the maximum sentence from 60 days in jail to a year in jail for two or more offenses. It will also institute a minimum mandatory sentence of ten days in jail for three or more offenses. Not written exclusively for immigrants, but it is clear that the governor and Congress created this to impact immigrants disproportionately based on press releases, etc.
Creates a crime for entering and remaining in Oklahoma without authorization. It is a misdemeanor with subsequent violations being felonies.

Notwithstanding any other provision of the Oklahoma Community Sentencing Act, a defendant is not eligible for community sentencing if the defendant is charged with or convicted of an offense under Section 2 of this act.
A defendant is also not eligible for probation or delayed sentencing for a violation of this Act.
Mandates collection of all identifying data and cross-checking it with local, state, and federal databases. Allows the OK Bureau of Investigations to keep that information.
Mandates that jailers "shall attempt to determine if the prisoner is a legal resident of the US.” by asking person or checking documents or both."
Jailers must honor DHS detainers. They must immediately ask a judicial official for an order to hold the person for 48 hours unless ICE picks them up or rescinds the order.
Creates indemnity for all local and state officials for following this Act.

Requires jail/facility administrators to provide a yearly report to the Joint Legislative Oversight Committee on Justice and Public Safety. Includes:
queries made to ICE; Number of responses from ICE; Detainers received; Prisoners held for a full 48 hours; Rescinded retainers; Number of prisoners detained when they would have been eligible for release; Number of times ICE took custody of a prisoner from that facility.
Mandates jails/arresting law enforcement to collaborate to verify the citizenship status of the person and report the status of those unlawfully present to the DAs.

"Beginning January 1, 2025, the district attorneys general conference shall collect and analyze data from law enforcement agencies on the number of persons not lawfully present in the United States charged or convicted of a criminal offense in this state during the previous year. "
Creates a special commission to study and recommend on emergency housing programs and other shelter issues. The Office of Immigrants included in the committee.
Allows more money for the migrant housing crisis.
Mandates the office of refugees and immigrants (in conjunction with 5+ other offices) to release a monthly report about approved training programs.

Governor Stitt established the Oklahoma State Work Permits and Visas Task Force in response to the continued lack of action by the federal administration to address legitimate ways for global workforce to legitimately work in
Oklahoma without diminishing public safety. The Order charged the Task Force to evaluate and make recommendations to allow immigrants to have the visas, permits and documentation to pursue the American Dream, contribute to our workforce and for Oklahoma Universities and industries to safely and legally recruit and retain global
talent without fear of unnecessary deportation or separation from family.
Creates a state crime for anyone who is "not lawfully present in the United States."

Mandates state officials to use the Systematic Alien Verification Entitlements Program (SAVE Program) to cross-reference immigrant's information with USCIS's database if they apply for federal, state, or local public benefits in the state.

States that "any natural person who is not lawfully present in the United States shall not knowingly apply for a federal public benefit or a state or local public benefit by submitting a false document to any entity that administers the federal public benefit or the state or local public benefit." Deems that violation a Class 6 felony.

States that there is to be no probation, pardon, commutation, or suspension of sentence or release on any other basis for people who violate the section about illegally entering the state from another nation or remaining in the state unlawfully.

Creates a provision stating that it will be a class 4 felony for aliens who refuse to comply with an order to return to their nation. See "A. A person who is an alien commits refusal to comply with an order to return to a foreign nation if all of the following occur:
1. The person is charged with or convicted of an offense under this article.
2. A court, as applicable, issues an order pursuant to section 13-4295.03 for the person to return to the foreign nation from which the person entered or attempted to enter the United States or the person's nation of origin.
3. The person refuses to comply with the order."
The state will grant the immigrant an opportunity to willingly return to their foreign nation via "an order to return to a foreign nation."
Provides civil immunity for state/local public entities, officials, employees, and contractors for following the law set forth in this legislation.

States that "Any natural person who is not lawfully present in the United States shall not knowingly submit false information or documents to an employer to evade detection of employment eligibility under the e-verify program." Creates a Class 1 misdemeanor offense for first-time offenders and a class 6 felony for subsequent offenses.

The county health officer shall, at least annually, investigate health and sanitary conditions in a county jail, publicly operated detention facility in the county, and private work furlough facility and program established pursuant to Section 1208 of the Penal Code
Allows undocumented people in Colorado to apply for state IDs. Sets requirements for those applications/processes.
ASPIRA Consent Decree in 1974 declared a right to translational bilignual education and English as a Second Language for NYC students.

Penalties can be imposed on employers who, for the purpose of concealing violations of state wage, benefit, or tax laws, disclose or threaten to disclose to a public body an employee's immigration status
Extends voting rights in local D.C. elections to non-citizens regardless of documentation
Expands MinnesotaCare (state healthcare) eligibility to include undocumented noncitizens (page 695 line 22)
Repeals a portion of SF2995 by revoking MinnesotaCare eligibility for undocumented noncitizens
Allows noncitizens to vote in local D.C. elections

Penalties can be imposed on employers who, for the purpose of concealing violations of state wage, benefit, or tax laws, disclose or threaten to disclose to a public body an employee's immigration status

localLaws.description: Effective September 2, 1997, “Clarion County Corrections will not hold Immigration detainees based on ICE detainers only."
Lebanon County prison revised its policy language in 2008, barring the prison from honoring ICE detainers. The Lebanon County Prison Board voted to rescind the language in May 2017.
Limits on detainers

The Bedford County Correctional Facility has a written policy prohibiting BCCF from honoring ICE detainers. Bedford County will not hold individuals for ICE without a court order authorized by a judge.

An email directive from a Berks County Jail official states: “ICE Officers will no longer have two business days to transport an inmate once all open charges are discharged. They will only have 4 hours from the time you notify them that the inmate is turned over into their custody and ready for transport to pick them up.” Under the current directive, a supervisor within the jail system receives notification every time an individual is turned over to ICE.
Police Dept will not honor detainers without warrant
Bucks County will not honor ICE detainers.
Effective September 9, 2014, Butler County Prison’s policy is “to not accept I.C.E. detainers as the sole holding or committing authority of any inmate(s).”
The Butler County Prison shall not use on duty time or Butler County Prison resources responding to ICE inquiries or communicating with ICE regarding an inmate’s custody status or release date.”
The Butler County Prison shall not permit ICE agents access to prison facilities or to any person in custody for investigative interviews or other investigative purposes.

Chester County Prison policy states it “will accept ICE detainers served by ICE agents on a suspected illegal alien” but “will not detain individuals or maintain continuing custody solely based on an ICE detainer.”
Can't arrest for immigration purposes unless warrant exists or other criminal violation is observed.

A privately run company, Community Education Centers (CEC), is responsible for correctional services at the George W. Hill Correctional Facility in Delaware County. Dated August 5, 2014, CEC’s policy is to not hold individuals based solely on ICE detainers. CEC was unwilling to turn over the policy because CEC considers it to be “proprietary information,” but noted that arrangements can be made for “in person” review of the policy.

Lehigh County’s policy states: “Once the subject of the detainer is not otherwise detained, the Lehigh County Department of Corrections shall release the subject of the detainer from County custody, unless the Lehigh County Department of Corrections receives a judicially-issued detainer, warrant or order.”
Lycoming County prison will not hold individuals based on ICE detainers alone.
Montgomery County Correctional Facility’s policy states that they will not honor ICE detainer requests.
Montour County Prison’s policy states: “Montour County does not honor ICE detainers, as set forth by law.”
Perry County Prison will not honor ICE detainers.
Perry County Prison shall not permit ICE agents access to County facilities or to any person in County custody

September 2, 2014, the Deputy Warden of Security of Westmoreland County Prison issued a memo stating that ICE detainers will not be accepted unless accompanied by a judicially authorized warrant or court order. If ICE detainers do not meet these requirements, they are sent back to the ICE agent.
Pike County Correctional Facility’s Standard Operating Procedure states that ICE detainers are not acceptable commitment paperwork nor can they be placed as a valid hold.
Will not honor ICE detainers without court order as part of a settlement agreement. “Davila v. Northern Regional Joint Police Board” (July 11, 2015).

Howard County had a jail space contract with ICE from 1995-2020. In 2020, Howard County updated its policy to only hold ICE detainees convicted of a violent crime at the Howard County Detention Center.
ICE expelled from Nassau County Correctional Center as of 2019
Sanctuary City policy

3/06-Municipal Code of Chicago, IL - 2-173-020. Executive Order 89-6 (4/89).  No city agent or agency shall request information about or otherwise investigate or assist in the investigation of the citizenship or residency status of any person, unless such inquiry or investigation is required by statute, ordinance, federal regulation, or court decision. Directs city agents and agencies to refrain from disseminating information regarding the citizenship or residency status of any person unless required to do so by legal process. City agents and agencies cannot condition the provision of city benefits, opportunities, or services on matters related to citizenship or residency status, unless required to do so by law, federal regulation, or court decision. Requires all applications, questionnaires, and interview forms relating to city benefits, opportunities, or services to delete any questions regarding individuals’ immigration status, if these questions are not required by law, regulation, or court decision.

ARTICLE 19:  On motion of Petitioner Leonard Solomon, duly seconded, it was voted 159 yes to 110 no, in keeping with current practices, that the town funds and other resources be refrained, not be used in taking action against immigrants, unless otherwise presented with a criminal warrant or other evidence of probable cause as required by the Fourth Amendment of the United States Constitution; and to see if the Town will request the Dennis Selectmen to advocate for the civil liberties and human rights of all Dennis residents and visitors regardless of race, ethnicity, religion, ability, sexual and gender identity, national origin or citizenship and immigration status.

Article 35. Non-binding Resolution of the Town   Petitioner L. Michael Hagar made a motion, which was seconded, that the town request the Eastham  Board of Selectmen to authorize all town officials to refrain from using town funds and other resources  to enforce federal immigration law unless presented with a criminal warrant or other evidence of  probable cause, as specified in Article 35 of the warrant, and further, that the town request the Board of  Selectmen to protect the civil liberties and human rights of all Eastham residents and visitors as specified  in Article 35 of the warrant.  

After considerable debate a motion was made and seconded to call the question.

) Q: Does the minimum wage law apply regardless of an employee’s Immigration status? A:  Employees are covered under the minimum wage ordinance regardless of their immigration status. The Minneapolis Department of Civil Rights will process an employee's report of suspected violation without regard to his or her immigration status. An employee filing a report will not be questioned about his or her immigration status. Additionally, if an employer were to suggest to a person that, if they file a wage claim or complaint, the employer will report suspected citizenship or immigration status of an employee or a family member to a government agency, this would be considered retaliation, which is also a violation of the ordinance.”

) Q: Does the minimum wage law apply regardless of an employee’s Immigration status? A:  Employees are covered under the minimum wage ordinance regardless of their immigration status. The Minneapolis Department of Civil Rights will process an employee's report of suspected violation without regard to his or her immigration status. An employee filing a report will not be questioned about his or her immigration status. Additionally, if an employer were to suggest to a person that, if they file a wage claim or complaint, the employer will report suspected citizenship or immigration status of an employee or a family member to a government agency, this would be considered retaliation, which is also a violation of the ordinance.”

Pulkrabek said at the meeting that his office does not enforce immigration laws, which fall under the purview of the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement Agency.

"My policy has been pretty clear on that," Pulkrabek said. "We’re not going to participate in raids with ICE. That’s not our responsibility."

Sheriff’s deputies also do not ask for people's immigration status or honor voluntary detainer requests from ICE that ask local jails to hold undocumented persons past their release date, Pulkrabek said."We don’t ask immigration status. If we have a victim of a crime, we want to do what we can to assist that person," he said.

However, the statement recognizes that the sheriff's office will comply if presented with a warrant, and will provide information and participate in criminal investigations.

Article 18.   Petitioned Article –Non-binding Resolution - Petition for Safe Communities Act.  To see if the Town will vote to request that Selectmen authorize all Town officials to refrain from using Town funds and other Town resources for the enforcement of federal immigration laws to the extent permissible by law, and unless presented with a criminal warrant or other evidence of probable cause as required by the 4th Amendment of the US Constitution (nonbinding resolution).  And, furthermore to see if the Town will vote to request that the Selectmen protect the civil liberties and human rights of all residents and visitors regardless of race, ethnicity, religion, ability, sexual and gender identity, national origin or citizenship and immigration status.  Or take any other action relative thereto.

The King County sheriff's office shall not request specific documents relating to a person's civil immigration status or otherwise initiate inquiry, for the sole purpose of determining whether the person has violated federal civil immigration laws.

The King County Sheriff’s Office or the Department of Adult and Juvenile Detention shall not carry out a civil arrest or hold a person after the release date set by a court based on an administrative warrant separately or in combination with an immigration detainer

Except when required by the law or a warrant, county employees/agents shall not disclose personal information, including place or birth, household members, the services received or the release date for any person. However, in order to comply with federal law, county employees/agents are permitted, but are not required to, share information about an individual’s citizenship or immigration status with immigration enforcement authorities.

Seattle’s Cannabis Employee Job Retention Ordinance, Seattle Municipal Code (SMC 8.38) addresses job insecurity in the cannabis industry by ensuring covered employees have information about ownership changes and requiring new employers to retain covered employees for a 90 day-transition period. 

 

7. Does an employee’s immigration status impact coverage or application of the law? 

No, immigration status does not impact coverage or application of the law. As a matter of policy, the City of Seattle does not ask about the immigration status of anyone using City services. Read OLS’ Commitment to Immigrant and Refugee Communities for more information. 

 

41. Does the law prohibit retaliation? 

Yes. Retaliation is illegal. Employers are prohibited from taking an adverse action against employees who assert or exercise their rights in good faith. 

These rights include (but are not limited to): 

▪ Asking questions about the law or the rights given by the law, 

▪ Informing someone about potential or actual violations of the law, 

▪ Filing a complaint with the Office of Labor Standards or participating in an investigation about potential or actual violations of the law, 

▪ Talking to the Office of Labor Standards or coworkers about the rights granted by this law, and 

▪ Informing other employees about their rights. 

An employee is still protected from retaliation even if they are mistaken about the right afforded. 

 

42. What is considered an adverse action? 

An adverse action is some action that negatively impacts any aspect of employment, including pay, work hours, responsibilities, or other material change in the terms or condition of employment. 

Some examples of adverse actions include: denying a job or promotion, demoting, terminating, failing to rehire after a seasonal interruption of work, threatening, penalizing, engaging in unfair immigration-related practice, filing of a false report with a government agency, changing employment status, or unlawfully discriminating against an employee. 

H. Waivers 

43. Can an individual employee waive their rights to the protections of this law? 

No. Individual

Vehicles that drop off passengers without filling out an application five days in advance and coordinating with Woodstock will face fines of $10,000 and $750 per passenger, in addition to impoundment.
Created the position of Immigrant and Refugee Liaison.
Celebrated the state's first Afghan Community center.
Established the Office of Immigrant and Refugee Affairs (OIRA) within the office of the City Mayor.

The Immigration Advisory Board (IAB) was established in 2019 to review and evaluate existing policies and make specific recommendations related to immigration to the mayor and City Council. It was dissolved in 2024 by the Bellingham City Council.

Replaced the IAB with the Keep Washington Working group. The purpose of the work group is to research, discuss and arrive at a shared
understanding of the City’s actions to achieve and maintain ongoing compliance with

"No employee of Concord shall inquire about the immigration status of an individual, including a crime victim,
a witness, or a person who calls or approaches the police, or any other member of the public with whom the
employee has contact, unless necessary to investigate criminal activity by that individual."

"No police officer of Concord shall arrest, detain, or continue to detain a person based solely on the belief
that the person is not present legally in the United States or that the person has committed a civil
immigration violation."

"No police officer of Concord shall arrest, detain, or continue to detain a person based on an immigration
detainer, federal administrative warrant, or any other such order or request in any form whatsoever, unless
such detainer or warrant is accompanied by a judicial warrant, and where: a) there is probable cause to
believe that the individual has illegally re-entered the country after a previous removal; and b) the individual
has been convicted at any time of a violent felony, terrorism-related offense, trafficking in individuals or
drugs, or participation in a criminal organization using violence."

"No police officer or employee of Concord shall respond to any ICE notification request by providing any
federal agent or agency information about an individual’s incarceration status, hearing information, length of
detention, home address, or personal information, but may provide information regarding citizenship or
immigration status."

"No police officer or employee of Concord shall perform the functions of an immigration officer, nor shall any
department of the Town of Concord use Town funds, resources, facilities, property, equipment or personnel
to assist in the enforcement of federal civil immigration laws. Nothing in this section shall prevent an officer,
employee or department from lawfully discharging duties in compliance with and in response to a lawfully
issued judicial warrant, judicial subpoena, or judicial detainer. "
San Diego opened a trolley station welcome center.
Ensures equitable access and inclusive access to all residents, including immigrants, to the City's governmental programming, services, and processes.
Promote and support the elimination of barriers to both child and adult education for immigrants.

No official or employee of the City may inquire into an individual's citizenship or immigration status, unless required to do so by federal law, state law, this ordinance, court order or unless such inquiry is reasonably necessary in the ocurse of a criminal investigation.

No official or employee of the City shall request, record, or access in government records the citizenship or immigration status of an individual, unless required to do so by federal law, state law, regulation, judicial warrant, court ofder or subpoena, or unless such inquiry is reasonably necessary in the course of a criminal investigation.

No official or employee of the City shall disclose to any person or entity the citizenship or immigration status of an individual, unless required to do so by federal law, state law, regulation,  court order or subpoena, or unless such inquiry is reasonably necessary in the course of a criminal investigation or has been authorized by the individual or their legal guardian.

The City shall not retain information related to an individual's citizenship or immigration status, unless required to do so by federal law, state law, this ordinance, or court order unless reasonably required to do so in the course of a criminal investigation.

Officials or employees of the City shall not utilize City resources ... to provide any information to immigration enforcement officials, unless the employee's action is expressly authorized under this Ordinance.

Officials or employees of the City shall not threaten, coerce, or intimidate anyone based on their citizenship or immigration status, actual or perceived, or the actual or perceived citizenship or immigration status of a member of the person's household.

When members of the Police Bureau are assigned as task force officers to DHS investigations, they may work with immigration officers while conducting other law enforcement activities related to violations of criminal law. However, under no circumstances shall a Lancaster City Police Bureau member, or other official or employee, whether assigned to a task force or not, have the authority to enforce administrative violations of immigration law or otherwise assist in the enforcement of civil immigration law.
Accept "matricula" cards as IDs
Nothing in this Ordinance shall prohibit Lancaster City Police Department from approving certification requests for crime victims or witnesses applying for U ot T visas, or other benefits.

Any person who alleges a violation of Section 3 of Ordinance by a member of the Bureau of Police may file with the Police Bureau a civilian complaint for investigation in accordance with the Lancaster Bureau of Police polices and procedures.

public servants cannot inquire about immigration status in most instances. only allowed if: relevant in a criminal investigation, determining flight risk of anyone arrested of a crime that could carry over 93 days in jail; checking on consular notification requirements, if there is a judicial warrant, if required by another program for any benefit; if hiring the person; if in litigation or subpoenaed;

The City Administrator shall provide an annual report to City Council of all violation complaints of this chapter and their disposition. The requirement for the annual report shall expire four years from the date of ordinance adoption unless extended by an act of Council.
police officers cannot ask about immigration to: check compliance with federal law; cannot ask about immigration status to a victim, witness, or anyone else seeking police service

police officers are expressly permitted to solicit information concerning immigration status from the subject of an investigation only when relevant to the investigation or prosecution of a violent criminal offense, or when processing an arrested person on suspicion of committing a violent felony as defined in Section 23-8-2 of this Code.
Police officers may not treat anyone differently if they use a passport or municipal ID as identification.
KC 24-25 Budget added $1M of funding for a new Refugee and Immigrant Services program. For short term rehousing, health care and job training programs for refugees and migrants.
Mayor's bill would seek to evict any migrants who have stayed over 60 days in homeless shelters.

The measure will require the Department of Family and Support Services (DFSS) to share reports that would include anonymized data on evicted migrants’ age, gender, country of origin, original eviction dates and length of shelter stay. The data will be made public in the Chicago Data Portal.
The ordinance also requires DFSS to publish biweekly reports on how many evicted migrants are returning to shelters from the landing zone; grievance reports and resolution data and information on how many migrants received extensions on their stays from Johnson’s updated 60-day shelter policy.

$1.5 million to establish an Immigration Law Clinic at the Louis D. Brandeis School of Law in fiscal 2025. The clinic will help Cuban immigrants with paperwork so they can more quickly get jobs in Kentucky. Largely aims at the Cuban population but is not exclusive.

NYC has two bilingual education programs for those learning English as a New Language - Dual Language Bilingual Education (DLBE): The goal of this program is for your child to read, write, and speak in both languages. After your child is no longer an English Language Learner (ELL), your child will stay in the program to learn and improve both languages. 
Transitional Bilingual Education (TBE): In these programs, as your child gets better in English, they will spend more time learning in English and spend less time learning in the home language. When your child is no longer an English Language Learner (ELL), they leave the program.
